perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the State of California applicable to contracts made and to be performed wholly within such State, and without regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof. Any suit brought hereon, whether in contract, tort, equity or otherwise, shall be brought in the state or federal courts sitting in Los Angeles County, California, the parties hereto hereby waiving any claim or defense that such forum is not convenient or proper. Each party hereby agrees that any such court shall have in personam jurisdiction over it, consents to service of process in any manner prescribed or authorized by California Law, and agrees that a final judgment in any such action or proceeding shall be conclusive and may be enforced in other jurisdictions by suit on the judgment or in any other manner specified by Law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the State of California applicable to contracts made and to be performed wholly within such State, and without regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof.
perturbation.explanation: I removed the clause specifying that lawsuits must be brought in California. This is an important part of the governing law clause. By deleting the specification of venue, this reduces enforceability by making it less clear where suits should be brought.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Code of Civil Procedure § 395 specifies the proper county for trial in various actions, including contract cases. By removing the explicit venue clause, the modified text creates ambiguity and potential conflict with the statutory venue provisions. This can make enforcing the contract more difficult as parties could dispute the appropriate venue.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 4. OF THE PLACE OF TRIAL, RECLASSIFICATION, AND COORDINATION OF CIVIL ACTIONS [392 - 404.9] ( Heading of Title 4 amended by Stats. 1999, Ch. 344, Sec. 6. ) CHAPTER 1. Place of Trial [392 - 403] ( Heading of Chapter 1 amended by Stats. 1999, Ch. 344, Sec. 7. ) 395. (a) Except as otherwise provided by law and subject to the power of the court to transfer actions or proceedings as provided in this title, the superior court in the county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action. If the action is for injury to person or personal property or for death from wrongful act or negligence, the superior court in either the county where the injury occurs or the injury causing death occurs or the county where the defendants, or some of them reside at the commencement of the action, is a proper court for the trial of the action. In a proceeding for dissolution of marriage, the superior court in the county where either the petitioner or respondent has been a resident for three months next preceding the commencement of the proceeding is the proper court for the trial of the proceeding. In a proceeding for nullit
perturbation.justification: The original text specified venue in Los Angeles County, California. The changed text removes this venue specification. CCP 395(a) states the proper court for trial is the superior court in the county where the defendants reside, unless otherwise provided by law. By removing the original venue clause, the contract introduces ambiguity about where a suit can be brought, which creates a potential conflict with CCP 395(a). This ambiguity makes enforcing the contract potentially more difficult, as parties may dispute the appropriate venue.

perturbation.original_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or any agreements or instruments relating hereto or delivered in connection herewith or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope or applicablility of this agreement to arbitrate, will at the request of any party be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles, California before three (3) arbitrators under the rules of the JAMS. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrators may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief in pursuit of a provisional or ancillary remedy shall not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or any agreements or instruments relating hereto or delivered in connection herewith or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope or applicablility of this agreement to arbitrate, will at the request of any party be determined by arbitration under mutually agreeable procedures. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrators may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief in pursuit of a provisional or ancillary remedy shall not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: I removed the location and arbitration rules which makes the arbitration clause less specific and potentially unenforceable. The term 'mutually agreeable procedures' is vague and does not provide a clear mechanism for arbitration. This reduction in enforceability is problematic under the Federal Arbitration Act.
perturbation.law_explanation: 9 U.S. Code § 4 of the Federal Arbitration Act requires the arbitration to proceed within the district where the arbitration agreement specifies. By removing the location (Los Angeles, California) and the established rules of the JAMS, the clause becomes less definitive regarding the process and location of arbitration, potentially conflicting with the FAA's requirements for enforcing arbitration agreements.

Failure to arbitrate under agreement; petition to United States court having jurisdiction for order to compel arbitration; notice and service thereof; hearing and determination A party aggrieve
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the specific location (Los Angeles, California) and the established rules of JAMS for arbitration, replacing them with 'mutually agreeable procedures.' This change creates a legal contradiction because it undermines the enforceability of the arbitration agreement. The Law Explanation points to 9 U.S. Code § 4, which requires arbitration to proceed within the district specified in the agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to 9 USC 4, concerning failure to arbitrate under agreement; petition to United States court having jurisdiction for order to compel arbitration; notice and service thereof; hearing and determination. This demonstrates that removing the specified location and rules conflicts with the FAA's requirements for enforcing arbitration agreements, as it introduces ambiguity about where and how arbitration should occur, potentially hindering a court's ability to compel arbitration.

perturbation.original_text: In any suit, action, arbitration or other proceeding to interpret or enforce this Agreement, the prevailing party therein shall, in addition to any other award of damage or other remedy, be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.
perturbation.changed_text: In any suit, action, arbitration or other proceeding to interpret or enforce this Agreement, the prevailing party may be awarded attorneys' fees, at the discretion of the judge or arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: I modified the attorney's fees clause to make it discretionary rather than mandatory. In California, some statutes provide for mandatory attorney's fees in certain types of cases. By making it discretionary, this could conflict with such statutes. This change creates uncertainty about the recovery of attorney's fees, potentially impacting a party's willingness to enforce the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Code of Civil Procedure § 1021 generally allows parties to agree on attorney's fees. However, certain statutes outside of this section mandate attorney fee awards to the prevailing party in specific types of cases (e.g., consumer protection cases). By making the fee award discretionary, the modified clause could conflict with those specific statutory provisions mandating fee awards, reducing enforceability in cases where such statutes apply.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 14. OF MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS [989 - 1062.34] ( Title 14 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 6. Of Costs [1021 - 1038] ( Chapter 6 enacted 1872. ) 1021. Except as attorney’s fees are specifically provided for by statute, the measure and mode of compensation of attorneys and counselors at law is left to the agreement, express or implied, of the parties; but parties to actions or proceedings are entitled to their costs, as hereinafter provided. (Amended by Stats. 1986, Ch. 377, Sec. 2.)
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates attorney's fees for the prevailing party, while the modified version makes it discretionary. This directly contradicts the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation which state that some statutes mandate attorney's fees in specific cases. CCP 1021 states that attorney's fees are determined by agreement unless specifically provided by statute. The modified contract creates a contradiction when a statute mandates attorney's fees, but the contract makes them discretionary.
